events.text: Edward Jackson, 63-year-old Black male, Never smoker. Lives independently. ECOG performance status 1.
Diagnosed with High-grade urothelial carcinoma of the Urinary bladder. Clinical staging: T3bN0M0, AJCC Stage IIIA. ICD-10: C67.9. Stage III muscle-invasive urothelial carcinoma of the bladder.
Pathology specimen received for initial biopsy.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for baseline staging.
Clinical visit for initial oncology consultation.
NGS report received for molecular profiling.
Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: Cisplatin 70 mg/m2 day 1 + Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 days 1, 8 q3w x 4 cycles. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 1 nephrotoxicity. Aggressive IV hydration. Creatinine monitored. Dose adjustment per CrCl.
Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: Cisplatin 70 mg/m2 day 1 + Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 days 1, 8 q3w x 4 cycles. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: Cisplatin 70 mg/m2 day 1 + Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 days 1, 8 q3w x 4 cycles. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 immune-related rash. Topical clobetasol 0.05% BID. Mild, no need for systemic steroids.
Initiated neoadjuvant chemotherapy: Cisplatin 70 mg/m2 day 1 + Gemcitabine 1000 mg/m2 days 1, 8 q3w x 4 cycles. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
Developed grade 2 immune-related rash. Topical clobetasol 0.05% BID. Mild, no need for systemic steroids.
MRI pelvis with contrast performed for restaging after neoadjuvant chemotherapy.
Clinical visit for results review after neoadjuvant chemotherapy.

Underwent surgical resection: Radical cystectomy with bilateral pelvic lymph node dissection and ileal conduit urinary diversion. Estimated blood loss 250 mL. Length of stay 3 days. Uncomplicated postoperative course.
Pathology specimen received for surgical pathology.
Clinical visit for postoperative follow-up.
Initiated adjuvant immunotherapy: Nivolumab 240 mg q2w x 12 months. Cycle 1. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 1 immune-related thyroiditis. TSH elevated to 12.5. Levothyroxine 50 mcg daily started.
Initiated adjuvant immunotherapy: Nivolumab 240 mg q2w x 12 months. Cycle 2. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 2 assessment.
Initiated adjuvant immunotherapy: Nivolumab 240 mg q2w x 12 months. Cycle 3. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated adjuvant immunotherapy: Nivolumab 240 mg q2w x 12 months. Cycle 4. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 4 assessment.
Initiated adjuvant immunotherapy: Nivolumab 240 mg q2w x 12 months. Cycle 5. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Initiated adjuvant immunotherapy: Nivolumab 240 mg q2w x 12 months. Cycle 6. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 6 assessment.
Initiated adjuvant immunotherapy: Nivolumab 240 mg q2w x 12 months. Cycle 7. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 3 neutropenia. Treated with G-CSF (filgrastim 5 mcg/kg daily x 5 days). CBC monitored closely.
Initiated adjuvant immunotherapy: Nivolumab 240 mg q2w x 12 months. Cycle 8. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 8 assessment.
Initiated adjuvant immunotherapy: Nivolumab 240 mg q2w x 12 months. Cycle 9. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 1 nephrotoxicity. Aggressive IV hydration. Creatinine monitored. Dose adjustment per CrCl.
Initiated adjuvant immunotherapy: Nivolumab 240 mg q2w x 12 months. Cycle 10. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 10 assessment.
Developed grade 2 nausea. Managed with ondansetron 8 mg q8h PRN and dexamethasone 4 mg daily.
Initiated adjuvant immunotherapy: Nivolumab 240 mg q2w x 12 months. Cycle 11. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Developed grade 2 immune-related rash. Topical clobetasol 0.05% BID. Mild, no need for systemic steroids.
Initiated adjuvant immunotherapy: Nivolumab 240 mg q2w x 12 months. Cycle 12. Patient tolerated treatment without immediate complications.
Clinical visit for cycle 12 assessment.
Developed grade 2 immune-related rash. Topical clobetasol 0.05% BID. Mild, no need for systemic steroids.
CT chest/abdomen/pelvis with contrast performed for restaging after adjuvant immunotherapy.
Clinical visit for results review after adjuvant immunotherapy.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
MRI pelvis with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.
MRI pelvis with contrast performed for surveillance imaging.
Clinical visit for surveillance follow-up.

documents.text:
PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Edward Jackson
MRN: patient_5827886187ac
Specimen ID: S2023-37398
Date of Procedure: 2023-12-04
Date of Report: 2023-12-07

SPECIMEN: Urinary bladder, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
High-grade urothelial carcinoma, well differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 4.3 cm
Margins: Positive at inked margin
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
CK7: Positive
CK20: Positive (focal)
GATA3: Positive
p63: Positive
CK5/6: Positive (focal)

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Edward Jackson - Urinary bladder" is a tan-pink tissue fragment measuring 4.3 x 3.0 x 2.1 cm. The cut surface reveals a indurated white mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A7.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a well differentiated neoplasm composed of cords and trabeculae of atypical cells with abundant cytoplasm and prominent nucleoli. Mitotic figures are identified (6 per 10 HPF). Focal necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows fibrosis.

Electronically signed by: Patricia Williams, MD
Department of Pathology

CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Edward Jackson
MRN: patient_5827886187ac
Date of Study: 2023-12-05

CLINICAL INDICATION: High-grade urothelial carcinoma of the urinary bladder. baseline staging.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Clear. No pulmonary nodules or masses. No pleural effusion.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: Irregular enhancing mass along the posterior wall of the bladder measuring 5.6 cm with likely extravesical extension.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Bladder mass with suspected extravesical extension. Muscle-invasive disease suspected.

Electronically signed by: Jennifer Walsh, MD
Department of Radiology

INITIAL CONSULTATION - Medical Oncology

Patient: Edward Jackson
MRN: patient_5827886187ac
Date: 2023-12-09
Provider: Dr. Carlos Mendez

CHIEF COMPLAINT: initial oncology consultation

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Mr./Ms. Jackson is a 63-year-old male who presents for initial evaluation of newly diagnosed high-grade urothelial carcinoma of the urinary bladder, IIIA. Patient presented with symptoms prompting workup. Stage III muscle-invasive urothelial carcinoma of the bladder. Pathology confirmed high-grade urothelial carcinoma. Molecular testing has been sent. Patient reports incidental finding on routine screening.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- coronary artery disease
- type 2 diabetes mellitus

SURGICAL HISTORY:
- Appendectomy (age 25)

SOCIAL HISTORY:
- Current smoker (1 ppd)
- Alcohol: No alcohol use
- Occupation: Engineer
- Lives with spouse and adult children

FAMILY HISTORY:
- Mother with breast cancer at age 62.

ALLERGIES: NKDA

MEDICATIONS:
- Metformin 1000 mg BID

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
Positive for: night sweats, mild peripheral edema, dizziness. Remainder of 14-point ROS reviewed and negative.

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:
Vitals: BP 118/84, HR 77, RR 14, Temp 98.7F, SpO2 98% on RA, Wt 84.0 kg
General: Well-nourished, alert, in no distress.
HEENT: No scleral icterus. Oropharynx clear.
Neck: Supple, no JVD, no cervical lymphadenopathy.
Lungs: Diminished breath sounds at right base. No wheezes.
CV: Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatosplenomegaly.
Extremities: No peripheral edema. Good pulses bilaterally.
Neuro: Alert and oriented. No focal neurological deficits.

LABORATORY RESULTS:
WBC 7.6, Hgb 11.2, Plt 120, Cr 0.77

IMAGING:
See separate imaging report.

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:
63-year-old male with IIIA high-grade urothelial carcinoma of the urinary bladder. initial oncology consultation.

1. High-grade urothelial carcinoma of the urinary bladder, Stage IIIA
   - Reviewed pathology and imaging with patient and family
   - Recommend neoadjuvant chemotherapy
   - Will discuss at multidisciplinary tumor board
   - Molecular profiling results pending
2. Supportive care
   - Nutrition consultation placed
   - Social work referral for psychosocial support
   - Port placement scheduled
3. Follow-up in 1-2 weeks to initiate treatment

Electronically signed by: Priya Sharma, MD
Medical Oncology

NEXT GENERATION SEQUENCING REPORT

Patient: Edward Jackson
MRN: patient_5827886187ac
Date of Report: 2023-12-22
Specimen: Urinary bladder (Resection)
Diagnosis: High-grade urothelial carcinoma
Panel: FoundationOne CDx (648 genes)

RESULTS SUMMARY:
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB): 13.2 mutations/Mb (Intermediate)
Microsatellite Status: MSS

CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT GENOMIC ALTERATIONS:
  FGFR3 p.S249C (c.746C>G) - Missense (Exon 7) [Clinically Significant]

ADDITIONAL PATHOGENIC ALTERATIONS:
  TP53 p.R282W - Missense (Exon 8)
  RB1 p.R445* - Nonsense (Exon 14)

VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE:
  ERBB2 p.S310F - VUS
  PIK3CA p.E545A - VUS

COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS:
  No significant copy number alterations detected

FUSIONS:
  No pathogenic fusions detected.

THERAPY ASSOCIATIONS:
  - FGFR3 S249C: FDA-approved erdafitinib for FGFR-altered advanced urothelial carcinoma.
  - Elevated TMB may support immunotherapy benefit.

Note: This test was performed using hybrid capture-based next generation sequencing.
Sequencing depth: mean 622x coverage.
Tumor content: 64%.

Electronically signed by: Maria Santos, MD, PhD
Molecular Pathology Laboratory

PATHOLOGY REPORT

Patient: Edward Jackson
MRN: patient_5827886187ac
Specimen ID: S2024-45433
Date of Procedure: 2024-04-16
Date of Report: 2024-04-20

SPECIMEN: Urinary bladder, excision/biopsy

FINAL DIAGNOSIS:
High-grade urothelial carcinoma, moderately differentiated.
Greatest tumor dimension: 6.8 cm
Margins: Negative (closest margin 0.5 cm)
Lymphovascular invasion: Present
Perineural invasion: Not identified

IMMUNOHISTOCHEMISTRY:
CK7: Positive
CK20: Positive (focal)
GATA3: Positive
p63: Positive
CK5/6: Positive (focal)

GROSS DESCRIPTION:
Received fresh labeled "Edward Jackson - Urinary bladder" is a firm tan tissue fragment measuring 6.8 x 4.8 x 3.4 cm. The cut surface reveals a indurated yellow-tan mass. Specimen entirely submitted in cassettes A1-A10.

MICROSCOPIC DESCRIPTION:
Sections show a moderately differentiated neoplasm composed of glands of atypical cells with scant cytoplasm and irregular nuclear contours. Mitotic figures are frequent (12 per 10 HPF). Necrosis is present. The surrounding stroma shows chronic inflammation.

Electronically signed by: Michael Torres, MD
Department of Pathology

CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH CONTRAST REPORT

Patient: Edward Jackson
MRN: patient_5827886187ac
Date of Study: 2024-11-18

CLINICAL INDICATION: High-grade urothelial carcinoma of the urinary bladder. restaging after adjuvant immunotherapy.

COMPARISON: None available.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT performed from skull base to iliac crest following administration of 100 mL Omnipaque 350 IV contrast.

FINDINGS:
LUNGS: Clear. No pulmonary nodules or masses. No pleural effusion.

MEDIASTINUM: No significant lymphadenopathy. Heart size normal.

LIVER: Homogeneous attenuation. No focal lesions.

PANCREAS: Normal in size and attenuation. No pancreatic mass or ductal dilation.

KIDNEYS: Normal in size bilaterally. No hydronephrosis or renal masses.

PELVIS: Irregular enhancing mass along the lateral wall of the bladder measuring 5.7 cm with likely extravesical extension.

BONES: No suspicious osseous lesions. Degenerative changes of the lumbar spine.

IMPRESSION:
1. Bladder mass with suspected extravesical extension. Muscle-invasive disease suspected.
